name: Fudgy Chocolate Layer Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup unsweetened cocoa powder, less one tablespoon
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line the bottom of two 9-inch round cake pans with wax paper.
Grease the paper and sides of the pans. Dust with flour.
Mix flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, beat butter, sugar, and brown sugar at medium speed until light
and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla.
At low speed, alternate adding the flour mixture and buttermilk to the butter
mixture just until blended. Divide the batter equally between prepared pans.
Bake cakes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean, 25 to 30 minutes.
Transfer the pans to wire racks to cool for 10 minutes. Turn cakes out onto the
racks and remove the paper. Turn layers over and let them cool completely.
To prepare frosting, beat butter and confectioner's sugar at medium speed until light
and fluffy. Add melted chocolate and vanilla; continue beating until shiny and smooth.
Place one cake layer on a serving plate; spread with frosting. Top with remaining
cake layer. Spread frosting on the top and sides of the cake. Let the cake stand
for at least 30 minutes before sprinkling with chocolate shavings and slicing.
